parameters.text:
=You are a proposal operations + compliance expert.

You receive:
1) TOR_TEXT = the full Terms of Reference text
2) EVALUATION_GRID_TEXT = optional evaluation grid / scoring text, not always available

Your job is to create ONE canonical tender JSON for downstream proposal automation.

STRICT RULES
- Output MUST be a SINGLE valid JSON object.
- No markdown. No commentary. No code fences.
- Use ONLY facts explicitly stated in the inputs.
- If something is missing, use null or [].
- Do NOT invent section IDs, evaluation weights, dates, deliverables, qualifications, or page limits.
- If the evaluation grid exists, use it to strengthen evaluation_alignment, evaluation_criteria, and scoring_structure.
- Preserve source language in short source_quotes when helpful, max 2 quotes per item, <=25 words each.
- required_response_structure must represent the human proposal sections.
- scoring_structure must represent the evaluator scoring structure.

RETURN EXACTLY THIS JSON SCHEMA:

{
  "rfp_id": string|null,
  "rfp_title": string|null,
  "client_org": string|null,
  "procurement_type": string|null,
  "project_number_cost_centre": string|null,

  "submission_deadline": {
    "date": string|null,
    "time_local": string|null,
    "timezone": string|null
  },

  "submission_method": {
    "channel": "Email"|"Portal"|"Physical"|"Other"|null,
    "details": string|null
  },

  "formatting_requirements": {
    "page_limit": number|null,
    "font_size": string|null,
    "language": string|null,
    "file_format": "PDF"|"DOCX"|"Other"|null,
    "other_constraints": string[]
  },

  "overall_expectations": string[],

  "evaluation_criteria": [
    {
      "criterion": string,
      "weight": number|null,
      "description": string|null,
      "source": "TOR"|"EVAL_GRID",
      "source_quotes": string[]
    }
  ],

  "required_response_structure": [
    {
      "section_id": string,
      "order": number,
      "title": string,
      "is_mandatory": boolean|null,
      "page_or_word_limit": string|null,
      "required_content": string[],
      "evaluation_alignment": string[],
      "source_quotes": string[]
    }
  ],

  "scoring_structure": [
    {
      "group_code": string,
      "group_title": string,
      "group_weight": number|null,
      "parent_section_id": string|null,
      "items": [
        {
          "criterion_code": string,
          "criterion_title": string,
          "weight": number|null,
          "best_response_format": "narrative"|"table"|"annex"|"cv"|"mixed"|null,
          "likely_evidence_needed": string[]
        }
      ],
      "source_quotes": string[]
    }
  ],

  "work_packages": [
    {
      "wp_no": string|null,
      "title": string,
      "objective": string|null,
      "deliverables": [
        {
          "deliverable_no": string|null,
          "title": string,
          "tasks": string[],
          "bottom_line": string|null,
          "source_quotes": string[]
        }
      ],
      "expert_days_estimate": number|null,
      "source_quotes": string[]
    }
  ],

  "milestones_and_period": {
    "period_of_assignment": {
      "start": string|null,
      "end": string|null
    },
    "milestones": [
      {
        "name": string,
        "deadline": string|null,
        "source_quotes": string[]
      }
    ]
  },

  "concept_requirements": {
    "technical_methodological": [
      {
        "code": string|null,
        "title": string,
        "must_cover": string[],
        "source_quotes": string[]
      }
    ],
    "project_management_musts": string[]
  },

  "personnel_requirements": {
    "roles": [
      {
        "role_name": string,
        "role_code": string|null,
        "tasks": string[],
        "qualifications": string[],
        "input_spec_days": number|null,
        "source_quotes": string[]
      }
    ]
  },

  "costing_and_travel_rules": {
    "rules": string[],
    "input_spec_table_notes": string[],
    "source_quotes": string[]
  },

  "compliance_checklist": [
    {
      "req_id": string,
      "requirement": string,
      "type": "Must"|"Should"|"Format"|"Eligibility"|"Submission",
      "mapped_to": string[],
      "source_quotes": string[]
    }
  ],

  "open_questions": string[]
}

SECTION EXTRACTION RULES
- required_response_structure must represent the proposal sections the bidder needs to write.
- section_id must be stable and compact: S1, S2, S3, etc.
- order must be numeric and sequential.
- evaluation_alignment must connect each section to explicit evaluator expectations if present.
- If the TOR defines concept / methodology / personnel / commercial sections, create them as separate sections.
- If the evaluation grid gives criteria that clearly map to a section, place that in evaluation_alignment for that section.

SCORING STRUCTURE RULES
- If an evaluation grid exists, capture the exact scoring hierarchy.
- Preserve criterion codes like 1.1, 1.1.1, 2.3, 2.3.4 exactly when present.
- Preserve explicit weights exactly when present.
- Map each scoring group to the most relevant parent_section_id.
- best_response_format should reflect what will best answer the criterion:
  - narrative
  - table
  - annex
  - cv
  - mixed

INPUTS

TOR_TEXT:
{{ $json.tor_text }}

EVALUATION_GRID_TEXT:
{{ $json.evaluation_grid_text }}

parameters.jsCode:
const headers = [
  "rfp_id","rfp_title","client_org","procurement_type",
  "submission_deadline_date","submission_deadline_time","submission_deadline_timezone",
  "submission_method_channel","submission_method_details",
  "section_id","order","title","is_mandatory","page_or_word_limit",
  "required_content","evaluation_alignment","source_quotes",
  "section_heading","score_weight_total","subsection_headings","criterion_level_guidance_json",
  "section_summary","what_evaluators_want","human_inputs_needed",
  "questions_for_humans","evidence_to_pull","drafting_outline",
  "section_win_themes","highlight_box_title","highlight_box_body",
  "suggested_tables_json","reusable_blocks_json",
  "executive_takeaways","proposal_strategy","win_themes","risks","go_no_go_flags",
  "clarification_questions","critical_human_contributions",
  "tender_insights_json","sheetId"
];

const row = {};
for (const h of headers) row[h] = h;

return [{ json: row }];

nodes.name: Append or update row in sheet
Delete rows or columns from sheet

parameters.text:
=You are a proposal coach + compliance QA. Your job is to design a HUMAN writer-facing blueprint for ONE proposal section.

You will receive SECTION_CONTEXT_JSON.
Use it to generate ONLY section-specific, actionable guidance.

STRICT OUTPUT RULES
- Output MUST be valid JSON.
- No markdown. No commentary. No code fences.
- Do NOT output bullets inside strings.
- For list fields, output JSON arrays of strings.
- questions_for_humans MUST be EXACTLY 5 items.
- Keep each item short, concrete, and answerable by a busy SME.
- Use section.required_content as the backbone.
- Use scoring_structure_for_section first when it exists.
- Make the section score-aware and content-specific, not generic.
- Use exact evaluation criterion codes and weights in headings where available.
- subsection_headings MUST reflect the evaluation structure when scoring_structure_for_section is provided.
- criterion_level_guidance MUST cover the concrete criteria in scoring_structure_for_section when provided.
- Identify one section-specific highlight box.
- Identify up to two suggested tables if tables would materially improve the response.
- Do NOT recommend reusable text blocks. Those are handled outside the AI.
- Never leave section_heading, section_summary, subsection_headings, criterion_level_guidance, what_evaluators_want, evidence_to_pull, or drafting_outline empty if the input contains enough information to infer them.

SECTION_CONTEXT_JSON:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.agent2_input) }}

Return a single JSON object with EXACT keys:

{
  "section_id": "string",
  "section_heading": "string",
  "section_summary": "string",
  "score_weight_total": number|null,
  "subsection_headings": [
    {
      "heading": "string",
      "criterion_codes": ["string"]
    }
  ],
  "criterion_level_guidance": [
    {
      "criterion_code": "string",
      "criterion_title": "string",
      "weight": number|null,
      "what_to_prove": ["string"],
      "what_not_to_do": ["string"],
      "best_format": "string"
    }
  ],
  "what_evaluators_want": ["string"],
  "human_inputs_needed": ["string"],
  "questions_for_humans": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "evidence_to_pull": ["string"],
  "drafting_outline": ["string"],
  "section_win_themes": ["string"],
  "highlight_box": {
    "title": "string",
    "body": "string"
  },
  "suggested_tables": [
    {
      "table_title": "string",
      "purpose": "string",
      "columns": ["string"]
    }
  ]
}

main.node: Append or update row in sheet
Delete rows or columns from sheet
